html:
[604]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 扶创起立 - Literally means 'to support the injured and stand up,' often used to describe helping someone who is ill or injured to rise.
  • 病勢似覺少減 - Means 'the condition seemed to slightly improve,' indicating a minor improvement in health.
  • 肉和药 - Refers to meat mixed with medicine, suggesting a medicinal preparation.
  • 煎而进之 - Means 'to boil and then give it to him,' describing the method of preparing and administering the medicine.
  • 翌日竟小愈 - Means 'the next day he was slightly better,' indicating a slight recovery.
  • 浃旬大安 - Means 'after ten days, he was greatly relieved,' suggesting a significant improvement after a period.
  • 弥月遂扶杖而起 - Means 'by the end of the month, he could walk with a cane,' indicating a full recovery.
  • 艳梅既不自言 - Means 'Yan Mei did not speak about it herself,' suggesting she kept her actions quiet.
  • 夫婿姑嫜亦罔知出于新妇之力也 - Means 'her husband and in-laws did not know it was due to the efforts of the new wife,' indicating the hidden contribution of the wife.
  • 学沫既痊 - Means 'Xue Mo was fully recovered,' indicating complete healing.
  • 益更束书不读 - Means 'he increasingly stopped reading books,' suggesting a decline in scholarly pursuits.
  • 时或出外游荡 - Means 'he sometimes went out to wander,' indicating a change in behavior.
  • 小试饮博之端 - Means 'he tried his hand at gambling and drinking,' suggesting a new interest in these activities.
  • 赖其母约束綦严 - Means 'relying on his mother's strict discipline,' indicating the mother's role in controlling his behavior.
  • 尚不敢过于驰骋 - Means 'he still dared not go too far,' suggesting a limit to his indulgence.
  • 又越二年，母已病卒 - Means 'after another two years, his mother had already fallen ill and died,' indicating the death of the mother.
  • 学沫乃肆无忌惮 - Means 'Xue Mo then acted without restraint,' suggesting a loss of discipline.
  • 日出与无赖者游 - Means 'he went out daily to hang out with ruffians,' indicating a negative association.
  • 举手以千金 - Means 'he spent a thousand gold coins at a stroke,' suggesting extravagant spending.
  • 缠头有百贯 - Means 'he gave a hundred strings of coins as a reward,' indicating generous tipping.
  • 凡祖父之经营 - Means 'all the efforts of his grandfather,' suggesting the inheritance of his grandfather's work.
  • 萱亲之积蓄 - Means 'the savings of his mother,' indicating the inheritance of his mother's savings.
  • 干仆之生殖 - Means 'the earnings of his servants,' suggesting the income from his servants.
  • 藏镪近千 - Means 'he had nearly a thousand gold coins,' indicating a large sum of money.
  • 朽索论万 - Means 'he had tens of thousands of strings of coins,' indicating a very large sum of money.
  • 尽输外府 - Means 'all were spent outside the home,' suggesting the complete depletion of resources.
  • 而家室为之一空 - Means 'and the household was left empty,' indicating the complete loss of wealth.
  • 浸及田宅 - Means 'gradually affecting the land and houses,' suggesting the spread of financial difficulties.
  • 人尽寒心 - Means 'people were all disheartened,' indicating widespread disappointment.
  • 独闺中者漠不为意 - Means 'only the woman in the inner room remained indifferent,' suggesting her lack of concern.
  • 所亲咸咎艳梅 - Means 'those close to him all blamed Yan Mei,' indicating the blame placed on her.
  • 以为同床秦越，痛痒弗关 - Means 'thinking that they were like Qin and Yue, sharing neither pain nor pleasure,' suggesting a lack of concern for each other.
  • 岂异日竟能独富哉 - Means 'how could she possibly become rich alone in the future,' indicating skepticism about her ability to become wealthy.
  • 艳梅闻而太息曰 - Means 'Yan Mei heard and sighed, saying,' indicating her reaction.
  • 虽非世上金夫，幸免冢中枯骨，何劝为 - Means 'although he is not a gold husband in the world, I am lucky to avoid being a skeleton in the tomb, what is there to advise,' indicating her resignation.
  • 益听其浪游 - Means 'she allowed him to wander more,' suggesting her acquiescence.
  • 且出奁具以供其挥霍 - Means 'and she also provided her dowry items to support his extravagance,' indicating her support for his spending.
  • 学洙以为贤 - Means 'Xue Zhou thought she was virtuous,' indicating his opinion of her.
  • 每向人津津乐道之 - Means 'he often spoke about it with great enthusiasm to others,' indicating his frequent praise of her.
  • 人以是亦薄艳梅 - Means 'people therefore also looked down on Yan Mei,' indicating the negative perception of her.
  • 未及二稔 - Means 'before two years,' indicating a short period.
  • 家无斗筲 - Means 'the household had no more than a few measures of grain,' indicating extreme poverty.
  • 夫妇遂徙居陋巷 - Means 'the couple then moved to a dilapidated alley,' indicating their relocation to a poor area.
  • 桑户棬枢 - Means 'mulberry door and bent door frame,' suggesting a simple and humble dwelling.
  • 竹屋低矮 - Means 'bamboo house, low and small,' indicating a modest living space.
  • 学洙始杜门不出 - Means 'Xue Zhou then closed the door and did not go out,' indicating his withdrawal from social life.
  • 乃自落魄以来 - Means 'since his fall from grace,' indicating the period of decline.
  • 一衣一食学洙皆仰给于妇 - Means 'every piece of clothing and meal for Xue Zhou depended on his wife,' indicating his reliance on her.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳梅 - A name that may have cultural significance, but no specific historical or cultural allusion is provided.
  • 学沫 - A name that may have cultural significance, but no specific historical or cultural allusion is provided.
  • 学洙 - A name that may have cultural significance, but no specific historical or cultural allusion is provided.

[605]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 毫无愠色 - Means 'without any sign of resentment or anger,' indicating a calm and patient demeanor.
  • 温言慰藉之 - Means 'to comfort with gentle words,' showing kindness and empathy.
  • 跋涉维艰 - Means 'to travel with great difficulty,' emphasizing the hardships of the journey.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 艳梅 - The name of a woman, likely a wife or partner, who is described as diligent and patient.
  • 学洙 - The name of a man, likely a husband or partner, who is described as dependent and lacking in skills.
  • 京秩 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the capital, indicating a position of authority.
  • 中州 - Refers to the central region of China, often associated with the Yellow River basin.
  • 赵州 - Refers to a specific place in China, likely a county or city.
  • 汝蔡之间 - Refers to a region between Ruzhou and Cai, indicating a specific geographical area.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳梅 - The name of a woman.
  • 学洙 - The name of a man.
  • 京秩 - A high-ranking official in the capital.
  • 中州 - The central region of China.
  • 赵州 - A specific place in China.
  • 汝蔡之间 - A region between Ruzhou and Cai.

[606]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 荏弱 - Classical term meaning 'delicate' or 'fragile,' often used to describe someone who is physically or emotionally weak.
  • 旅次 - Refers to a journey or travel, often used in the context of being away from home.
  • 舅氏 - Refers to one's uncle or maternal uncle, here used to describe the husband's uncle.
  • 下车未久 - Literally means 'not long after arriving,' used to indicate a short period of time since a new official has taken office.
  • 揭参 - Refers to the act of exposing corruption or misconduct, often in the context of official investigations.
  • 暮金 - A euphemism for bribes or late-night gifts, often given to officials.
  • 资斧将尽 - Literally means 'the funds are nearly exhausted,' used to describe a state of financial depletion.
  • 告助无门 - Means 'having no one to turn to for help,' indicating a sense of isolation and desperation.
  • 束手坐困 - Means 'to be helpless and trapped,' often used to describe a situation where one is unable to take action.
  • 先返 - Means 'to return first,' indicating that someone went back before others.
  • 耽延匝月 - Means 'delayed for a full month,' indicating a prolonged period of delay.
  • 衙署 - Refers to the official residence or office of a government official.
  • 吏役 - Refers to officials and their subordinates.
  • 刺史 - Refers to a provincial governor or administrator.
  • 避向都门旧居 - Means 'to retreat to the old residence in the capital,' indicating a move back to a safer place.
  • 召甥之价未回 - Means 'the money promised for the son-in-law has not returned,' indicating a financial loss.
  • 去赵之旌已远而莫可追矣 - Means 'the honor of being a son-in-law of Zhao has been lost and cannot be recovered,' indicating a significant social and financial setback.
  • 气绝心灰 - Means 'to lose all hope and spirit,' indicating a state of despair.
  • 悔妇言之不用 - Means 'regretting not listening to his wife's advice,' indicating a sense of remorse.
  • 无所之 - Means 'nowhere to go,' indicating a state of being stranded.
  • 资斧将尽 - Literally means 'the funds are nearly exhausted,' used to describe a state of financial depletion.
  • 告助无门 - Means 'having no one to turn to for help,' indicating a sense of isolation and desperation.
  • 束手坐困 - Means 'to be helpless and trapped,' often used to describe a situation where one is unable to take action.
  • 盍筹其所以处之 - Means 'why not consider what to do about it,' indicating a suggestion to find a solution.
  • 妙术 - Refers to a special skill or technique, often used in the context of magic or supernatural abilities.
  • 手爪养此口腹 - Means 'to earn a living with one's own hands,' indicating a self-sufficient lifestyle.
  • 筹其所以处之 - Means 'to consider what to do about it,' indicating a need to find a solution.
  • 择日设肆 - Means 'to choose a day to set up a stall,' indicating the decision to start a business.
  • 赁屋治具 - Means 'to rent a house and prepare the necessary tools,' indicating the preparation for a new venture.
  • 悬一幔于门 - Means 'to hang a curtain at the door,' indicating the setup of a shop.
  • 默坐其中 - Means 'to sit silently inside,' indicating a calm and focused state.
  • 往来传送 - Means 'to come and go, delivering items,' indicating the role of the husband in the business.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 艳梅 - The name of the wife, likely a common name in the context of the story.
  • 学洙 - The name of the husband, likely a common name in the context of the story.
  • 舅氏 - Refers to the husband's uncle, a common familial term.
  • 刺史 - Refers to a provincial governor or administrator, a common title in the context of the story.
  • 揭参 - Refers to the act of exposing corruption or misconduct, a common practice in the context of the story.
  • 暮金 - A euphemism for bribes or late-night gifts, a common practice in the context of the story.
  • 资斧 - Refers to funds or money, a common term in the context of the story.
  • 告助无门 - A common phrase used to describe a state of isolation and desperation.
  • 束手坐困 - A common phrase used to describe a situation where one is unable to take action.
  • 妙术 - Refers to a special skill or technique, often used in the context of magic or supernatural abilities.
  • 手爪养此口腹 - A common phrase used to describe a self-sufficient lifestyle.
  • 择日设肆 - A common phrase used to describe the decision to start a business.
  • 赁屋治具 - A common phrase used to describe the preparation for a new venture.
  • 悬一幔于门 - A common phrase used to describe the setup of a shop.
  • 默坐其中 - A common phrase used to describe a calm and focused state.
  • 往来传送 - A common phrase used to describe the role of the husband in the business.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳梅 - The name of the wife.
  • 学洙 - The name of the husband.
  • 舅氏 - The husband's uncle.
  • 刺史 - The provincial governor or administrator.
  • 暮金 - A euphemism for bribes or late-night gifts.
  • 资斧 - Funds or money.
  • 告助无门 - A common phrase used to describe a state of isolation and desperation.
  • 束手坐困 - A common phrase used to describe a situation where one is unable to take action.
  • 妙术 - A special skill or technique.
  • 手爪养此口腹 - A self-sufficient lifestyle.

[607]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 就問者 - Refers to those who ask questions or seek advice.
  • 辄书片纸，批而答之 - Means 'would write on a small piece of paper and answer with a brief note.'
  • 柳外之莺 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'the song of the oriole outside the willow,' symbolizing something pleasant or desirable that is unattainable.
  • 矧其他哉 - A rhetorical expression meaning 'how much more so for other things?' emphasizing the impossibility of obtaining even more desirable things.
  • 赵人初犹观望，渐且云集 - Means 'the people of Zhao initially hesitated but gradually gathered like a crowd.'
  • 旬日而户外之屦满矣 - Means 'within ten days, the floor outside was full of shoes,' indicating a large number of visitors.
  • 喧传，名噪远近 - Means 'the news spread widely, and her fame grew both near and far.'
  • 幔中仙之誉倾动簪绅 - Means 'the reputation of the 'woman in the curtain' caused a stir among the gentry.'
  • 盖不止士庶已也 - Means 'it was not just the common people who were impressed.'
  • 晨起坐幔中，以十人为率 - Means 'she would rise early and sit in the curtain, with ten people as a standard.'
  • 余皆辞以翌日 - Means 'the rest were told to come the next day.'
  • 青蚨百数 - Refers to a sum of money, with '青蚨' being a metaphor for coins.
  • 仪足饔飧 - Means 'enough to cover meals.'
  • 学洙疑之曰 - Means 'Xue Zhu questioned her, saying...'
  • 以子之能，富可立致 - Means 'with your ability, wealth could be easily acquired.'
  • 非予敢存奢望，何不多取锱铢，早还乡里 - Means 'I do not dare to have excessive hopes, but why not take more, to return home sooner?'
  • 自为限量如此 - Means 'to limit yourself in this way.'
  • 命之理微，圣人且罕言之 - Means 'the principle of fate is subtle, even sages rarely speak of it.'
  • 妾之神明 - Means 'my divine insight.'
  • 多则不验 - Means 'if there are too many, it will not be effective.'
  • 虚人来意耶 - Means 'would it be in vain for people to come?'
  • 此子之饰说耳 - Means 'this is just his embellished talk.'
  • 术苟甚神，安在不多多益善哉 - Means 'if the art is truly divine, why not take more and more?'
  • 益固强之 - Means 'Xue Zhu insisted even more strongly.'
  • 自朝至于日昃 - Means 'from morning until noon.'
  • 得钱近千 - Means 'he earned nearly a thousand coins.'
  • 诘旦，夫妇犹未起，暑雨滂沱 - Means 'the next day, the couple had not yet risen, and it was pouring with summer rain.'
  • 旁午，天始稍霁，来者甚稀 - Means 'at midday, the sky began to clear slightly, and the number of visitors was very few.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 赵人 - Refers to the people of Zhao, a state during the Warring States period.
  • 艳梅 - A character in the story, likely a fortune-teller or spiritual medium.
  • 学洙 - A character in the story, likely a scholar or seeker of knowledge.
  • 青蚨 - A metaphor for coins or money.
  • 饔飧 - Refers to meals, with '饔' meaning morning meal and '飧' meaning evening meal.
  • 簪绅 - Refers to the gentry or upper class.
  • 士庶 - Refers to scholars and common people.
  • 命金 - Refers to the fee paid for a reading or consultation.
  • 术 - Refers to the art or skill of fortune-telling or spiritual divination.
  • 神明 - Refers to divine insight or supernatural ability.
  • 虚人来意耶 - A rhetorical question about whether it would be in vain for people to come.
  • 饰说 - Refers to embellished or exaggerated talk.
  • 多多益善 - A proverb meaning 'the more, the better.'
  • 命之理微 - Refers to the subtle nature of fate.
  • 圣人 - Refers to sages or wise men.
  • 妾 - A humble term used by a woman to refer to herself.

[608]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至暮，重陰凝閉 - By dusk, heavy clouds gathered and closed in, describing a prolonged period of overcast weather.
  • 遂成淫霖 - And thus became a continuous downpour, where '淫霖' refers to an unceasing or excessive rain.
  • 十余日无少止息 - For more than ten days without any cessation, indicating a prolonged period of rain.
  • 人绝无乘槎问卜者 - No one ventured out on a raft to consult the oracle, using '乘槎' (riding a raft) as a metaphor for traveling or seeking omens.
  • 夫妇坐食其资 - The couple simply consumed their funds, indicating idleness or lack of action.
  • 枵腹一朝夕 - Fasted for a day and night, where '枵腹' means an empty stomach.
  • 艳梅益信有定数 - Yan Mei became even more convinced of fate's design, using '定数' to refer to predestined or fixed destiny.
  • 亟欲复其故辙 - Urgently wanted to return to their old path, where '故辙' is a metaphor for previous actions or methods.
  • 三日又获资数缗 - Three days later, they again obtained several strings of cash, where '缗' refers to a unit of currency (typically 1000 coins).
  • 高枕而眠 - Sleeping soundly with a high pillow, a classical idiom meaning to be at ease or complacent.
  • 席卷所有，且并其衣物，尽携而去 - He swept up everything, even their clothes, and took them all away, using '席卷' to emphasize the thoroughness of the theft.
  • 坏垣未及修整 - The broken walls had not yet been repaired, indicating neglect or lack of maintenance.
  • 宵人得以肆其志 - Night people were able to indulge their desires, where '宵人' refers to thieves or criminals.
  • 究亦造化使然也 - In the end, it was the will of Heaven, where '造化' refers to the natural order or fate.
  • 学洙大恸，愤不欲生 - Xue Zhu was overwhelmed with grief and filled with despair, unwilling to live, indicating deep emotional distress.
  • 言笑自若 - Spoke and laughed as usual, meaning to remain calm and composed.
  • 君不知命，宜其不免于贫 - You do not understand fate, so it is no wonder you remain poor, a statement about destiny and personal failure.
  • 妾遭媒妁之误，至今犹深恫于心 - I was deceived by the matchmaker, and to this day, it still deeply troubles me, indicating lasting emotional pain.
  • 非贫当殀 - It is not poverty that should cause death, where '殀' refers to an untimely or unnatural death.
  • 微妾祷于天帝，予今已少寡久矣 - Had it not been for my prayers to the Heavenly Emperor, I would have long been a widow, indicating a miraculous escape from misfortune.
  • 安得一寒彻骨，犹然倡随至是乎 - How could I endure such bitter cold and still remain by your side to this extent? A rhetorical question expressing devotion and suffering.
  • 君无怨焉可耳 - You have no cause for resentment, a statement of resignation or acceptance.
  • 因述其梦中天语 - She then recounted the words of Heaven from her dream, where '天语' refers to divine or celestial words.
  • 所造如斯，愈无首止之望 - With such misfortunes, there is even less hope of turning things around, where '首止' is an idiom for reversing a bad situation.
  • 善可回天乎 - Can virtue turn the heavens? A rhetorical question implying that moral conduct can change fate.
  • 彼苍固任人转圜者也 - The heavens do allow for human effort to change things, where '转圜' means to mend or adjust a situation.
  • 诚能力行阴骘 - If you truly practice good deeds, where '阴骘' refers to unseen or moral virtues that influence fate.
  • 天将别注禄籍 - The heavens will record your name in the register of fortune, where '禄籍' is a celestial ledger of merit and reward.
  • 以妾之能，固可相君旋里，且还尔素封矣 - With my abilities, I can certainly help you return to your hometown and restore your former estate, where '素封' refers to one's original wealth or status.

[609]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沉思良久 - Literally 'pondered for a long time,' indicating deep reflection.
  • 猝不能过 - Literally 'suddenly unable to cross,' describing an unexpected difficulty.
  • 水深湍急 - Literally 'deep and swift water,' emphasizing the dangerous nature of the river.
  • 距此里许 - Literally 'about a mile from here,' indicating a specific distance.
  • 别无桥梁 - Literally 'no other bridge,' highlighting the lack of infrastructure.
  • 立志 - Literally 'set one's mind to,' meaning to make a firm decision.
  • 负土担石 - Literally 'carry earth and stones,' describing the physical labor involved.
  • 鸠工架木 - Literally 'assemble workers and build wooden structures,' referring to the construction process.
  • 终岁之间 - Literally 'within a year,' indicating the timeframe.
  • 人即永免衣襦之患 - Literally 'people will forever be free from the suffering of cold,' suggesting long-term benefits.
  • 利济诚宏 - Literally 'the benefit is truly great,' emphasizing the positive impact.
  • 善不在此乎 - Literally 'isn't this good?' a rhetorical question affirming the goodness of the action.
  • 慨然曰 - Literally 'said with a sigh,' indicating a sense of resolve or determination.
  • 遂与艳梅谋 - Literally 'then planned with Yan Mei,' showing collaboration.
  • 每日食余启肆 - Literally 'every day after meals, open the shop,' indicating their daily routine.
  • 日晡便休 - Literally 'rest at dusk,' describing their work schedule.
  • 暇则前往营建 - Literally 'when free, go to construct,' indicating their commitment to the project.
  • 水浸胫骨 - Literally 'water soaking the shins,' describing the physical hardship.
  • 劳不知疲 - Literally 'working without knowing fatigue,' emphasizing their dedication.
  • 严寒之龟手皴足 - Literally 'cold hands and cracked feet in the severe cold,' describing the harsh conditions.
  • 盛暑之雨笠烟蓑 - Literally 'rain cap and smoke cloak in the hot summer,' describing protective gear.
  • 举所不恤 - Literally 'all without concern,' indicating their willingness to endure hardships.
  • 人有乐助者 - Literally 'those who are willing to help,' describing the community's support.
  • 反揖而去之 - Literally 'bow back and leave them,' indicating a polite refusal of assistance.
  • 夫妇又自兹茹素 - Literally 'the couple then began to eat vegetarian food,' describing a change in lifestyle.
  • 口食而外 - Literally 'except for what they eat,' indicating additional contributions.
  • 悉以庀材 - Literally 'all to gather materials,' showing their resourcefulness.
  • 物集而后召匠 - Literally 'gather materials and then call in craftsmen,' indicating a logical sequence of actions.
  • 攻木攻石 - Literally 'cut wood and stone,' describing the construction activities.
  • 斧凿之声盈于路 - Literally 'the sound of axes and chisels fills the road,' indicating the intensity of the work.
  • 通计经始至此 - Literally 'calculated from the start to now,' indicating the duration of the project.
  • 凡阅十月而桥成 - Literally 'in ten months, the bridge was completed,' indicating the timeline.
  • 初是溪虽深 - Literally 'at first, the stream was deep,' describing the initial state.
  • 有时亦仅没膝 - Literally 'sometimes it only reached the knees,' indicating variations in depth.
  • 人恒不能涉 - Literally 'people always could not cross,' emphasizing the difficulty.
  • 原其水性峻急 - Literally 'originally, the water was steep and swift,' explaining the cause.
  • 即盎盂之石 - Literally 'even small stones in a bowl,' indicating the size of objects that could be moved by the current.
  • 皆可激之使行 - Literally 'all could be stirred to move,' emphasizing the power of the current.
  • 膑脚刖足 - Literally 'crippled feet and legs,' describing severe injuries.
  • 立尚颠仆 - Literally 'standing still would still result in falling,' indicating the danger.
  • 遂无有创此议者 - Literally 'no one dared to propose this idea,' indicating the lack of initiative.
  • 自学洙之尽日立于水次 - Literally 'since Xuezhu stood by the water all day,' indicating his persistence.
  • 狂澜汹涌 - Literally 'wild waves surging,' describing the turbulent water.
  • 究无损伤 - Literally 'ultimately, no harm,' indicating his safety.
  • 人因以为神 - Literally 'people therefore considered him divine,' indicating the reverence he received.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 艳梅 - The name of the female character, likely a courtesan or a woman of low social status.
  • 学洙 - The name of the male character, likely a scholar or a person of higher social status.
  • 衣襦之患 - Refers to the suffering caused by cold, indicating the importance of warmth.
  • 利济诚宏 - Emphasizes the positive impact of the action, reflecting Confucian values of benevolence and social responsibility.
  • 茹素 - Refers to a vegetarian diet, often associated with religious or moral practices.
  • 庀材 - Refers to gathering materials, indicating resourcefulness and planning.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳梅 - The name of the female character.
  • 学洙 - The name of the male character.
  • 衣襦 - Refers to clothing, specifically warm clothing.
  • 利济 - Refers to the benefit or utility of the action.
  • 茹素 - Refers to a vegetarian diet.
  • 庀材 - Refers to gathering materials.
  • 攻木攻石 - Refers to cutting wood and stone.
  • 斧凿 - Refers to tools used in construction.

[610]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 徒杠已就 - Literal meaning: 'the simple bridge is completed.' Implies the bridge is ready for use.
  • 行旅欢然 - Literal meaning: 'the travelers are joyful.' Indicates a positive reaction to the bridge's completion.
  • 因号之以幔仙桥 - Literal meaning: 'they named it the 'Mantian Bridge.' 'Mantian' (幔仙) suggests a bridge that appears like a fairy's veil.
  • 纪其灵也 - Literal meaning: 'to record its miraculous nature.' Implies that the bridge is considered extraordinary or divine.
  • 学洙亦由是小康 - Literal meaning: 'Xuezhu also became relatively wealthy as a result.' Indicates an improvement in his financial situation.
  • 遂有薄积 - Literal meaning: 'and thus accumulated a small fortune.' Suggests a modest but significant amount of wealth.
  • 又越两岁 - Literal meaning: 'after another two years.' Indicates a passage of time.
  • 致钱百缗 - Literal meaning: 'he managed to save a hundred strings of cash.' '缗' (mǐn) refers to a unit of currency.
  • 乃作旋归之计 - Literal meaning: 'he then planned to return home.' Indicates his intention to go back.
  • 市二蹇卫 - Literal meaning: 'he bought two old horses.' '蹇' (jiǎn) refers to old or weak horses.
  • 载妻以行 - Literal meaning: 'to carry his wife with him.' Indicates that he brought his wife along on the journey.
  • 艳梅自至赵地 - Literal meaning: 'Yan Mei has been in Zhao territory.' Indicates her location.
  • 深自韬晦 - Literal meaning: 'she has deeply concealed herself.' Suggests that she has been keeping a low profile.
  • 从未以半面示人 - Literal meaning: 'she has never shown her face to anyone.' Indicates that she has not revealed herself to others.
  • 至是返辔 - Literal meaning: 'upon returning, she reined in her horse.' Indicates the start of her journey back.
  • 人甫识荆 - Literal meaning: 'people just began to recognize her.' Suggests that people started to notice her.
  • 虽年届三旬 - Literal meaning: 'though she is in her thirties.' Indicates her age.
  • 而花貌俨然 - Literal meaning: 'but her appearance is still as beautiful as a flower.' Suggests that she looks youthful and attractive.
  • 举惊为神仙 - Literal meaning: 'everyone was surprised by her beauty, thinking she was a fairy.' Indicates that people were amazed by her appearance.
  • 不敢仰视 - Literal meaning: 'they dared not look up at her.' Suggests that people were so impressed that they were intimidated.
  • 夫妇遂就道 - Literal meaning: 'the couple then set out on the road.' Indicates that they started their journey.
  • 不再徘徊 - Literal meaning: 'they did not hesitate.' Suggests that they were determined to continue.
  • 乃行未三日程 - Literal meaning: 'but they had only traveled for three days.' Indicates the short duration of their journey.
  • 学洙忽得暴疾 - Literal meaning: 'Xuezhu suddenly fell ill with a severe illness.' Indicates the onset of his illness.
  • 昏睡无知 - Literal meaning: 'he fell into a deep sleep, unconscious.' Suggests that he was in a coma.
  • 抚之若冰 - Literal meaning: 'touching him felt like touching ice.' Indicates that he was cold and unresponsive.
  • 竟卒于旅舍 - Literal meaning: 'he eventually died in the inn.' Indicates the place of his death.
  • 艳梅恸甚 - Literal meaning: 'Yan Mei was greatly grieved.' Indicates her deep sorrow.
  • 自恨其生之不辰 - Literal meaning: 'she regretted her unfortunate fate.' Suggests that she felt unlucky.
  • 百计维持 - Literal meaning: 'she tried every possible way to sustain him.' Indicates her efforts to save him.
  • 究无望于偕老 - Literal meaning: 'but there was no hope for them to grow old together.' Suggests that she realized they would not live long together.
  • 且居异乡 - Literal meaning: 'and being in a foreign land.' Indicates their location.
  • 只身谁倚 - Literal meaning: 'alone, who could she rely on.' Suggests her loneliness.
  • 遂哭之呕血 - Literal meaning: 'she cried so much that she vomited blood.' Indicates the intensity of her grief.
  • 绝而复生 - Literal meaning: 'she died but then revived.' Suggests a near-death experience.
  • 旅邸主人又以其死之甚遽 - Literal meaning: 'the inn owner was also alarmed by his sudden death.' Indicates the owner's reaction.
  • 不免干连 - Literal meaning: 'he could not avoid getting involved.' Suggests that the owner felt implicated.
  • 将首之于官而脱其累 - Literal meaning: 'he intended to report it to the authorities to clear himself of any responsibility.' Indicates the owner's intention to avoid blame.
  • 正纷然聚讼 - Literal meaning: 'the situation was chaotic with many people arguing.' Indicates a scene of confusion and dispute.
  • 观者如堵 - Literal meaning: 'the spectators were as dense as a wall.' Suggests a large crowd.
  • 忽一人排众直入 - Literal meaning: 'suddenly, a person pushed through the crowd and entered.' Indicates the arrival of someone.
  • 以寸缄予艳梅曰 - Literal meaning: 'gave her a short note and said.' Indicates the delivery of a message.
  • 前村一媪，闻娘子命理綦精 - Literal meaning: 'an old woman from the village heard that you have a very precise understanding of fate.' Indicates the old woman's knowledge.
  • 敬以此相浼 - Literal meaning: 'respectfully entrusting this to you.' Suggests a request for help.
  • 众闻而大恚 - Literal meaning: 'the crowd heard and became very angry.' Indicates their reaction.
  • 以为当此抢攘 - Literal meaning: 'thinking that at such a chaotic time.' Suggests the crowd's perception of the situation.
  • 何来是不急之务 - Literal meaning: 'why bring up such an urgent matter.' Indicates the crowd's frustration.
  • 将拳石交下 - Literal meaning: 'they threw punches and stones.' Indicates physical violence.
  • 以泄其不平 - Literal meaning: 'to vent their anger.' Suggests the crowd's intent.
  • 艳梅挥之曰 - Literal meaning: 'Yan Mei waved them off and said.' Indicates her response.
  • 是必有异 - Literal meaning: 'there must be something unusual about this.' Suggests her suspicion.
  • 展视焉则其师茅媪之造也 - Literal meaning: 'when she opened it, it was from her teacher, Mother Mao.' Indicates the source of the message.

  • 幔仙桥 - A poetic name for the bridge, suggesting a bridge that appears like a fairy's veil.
  • 赵地 - Refers to the territory of the state of Zhao during the Warring States period.
  • 三旬 - Refers to the age of thirty.
  • 花貌 - Refers to a beautiful appearance, often used to describe young women.
  • 神仙 - Refers to immortals or deities in Chinese mythology.
  • 命理 - Refers to the study of fate or destiny, often associated with divination and astrology.
  • 抢攘 - Refers to a chaotic or tumultuous situation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 使非予草数行 - Formal expression meaning 'If it were not for me writing a few lines,' implying that the action or event would not have occurred without the narrator's intervention.
  • 渠或夺艳梅之婚 - Literally 'He might have taken away Yan Mei's marriage,' suggesting a possible interference or disruption in Yan Mei's marital situation.
  • 妻汝以二女未可知也 - Formal expression meaning 'It is not impossible that he could have married you with two daughters,' conveying a speculative or hypothetical scenario.
  • 言次，旅邸主人与众皆心安 - Literally 'During the conversation, the innkeeper and everyone else felt at ease,' indicating a sense of reassurance or comfort.
  • 始各散去 - Means 'They then dispersed,' suggesting the end of a gathering or event.
  • 学洙设具款媪 - Literally 'Xuezhou prepared food to entertain the old woman,' indicating hospitality and respect.
  • 聚饮甚欢 - Means 'They gathered to drink and were very happy,' describing a joyful and convivial atmosphere.
  • 媪忽诘陆夫妇曰 - Literally 'The old woman suddenly questioned the couple Lu,' indicating a sudden and direct inquiry.
  • 仙乡乐乎？故乡乐乎？ - Rhetorical question meaning 'Is the fairyland more enjoyable than the homeland?' exploring the preference between an idealized place and one's own origins.
  • 二人同声俱以仙乡对 - Means 'Both answered in unison that the fairyland was more enjoyable,' indicating a unanimous response.
  • 探囊中，取出刀圭之药 - Literally 'She reached into her bag and took out a small amount of medicine,' describing the act of retrieving a substance.
  • 令艳梅服之 - Means 'She ordered Yan Mei to take it,' indicating a command or instruction.
  • 少与学洙曰 - Literally 'She spoke privately to Xuezhou,' suggesting a confidential or intimate conversation.
  • 渠身故自有宝也 - Means 'He indeed has a treasure within himself,' implying an inherent value or potential.
  • 更以余药饲二蹇 - Literally 'She gave the remaining medicine to the two donkeys,' indicating the distribution of a substance.
  • 遂亦不烦刍秣 - Means 'And they no longer needed fodder,' suggesting a miraculous or extraordinary effect.
  • 凌展同出逆旅 - Literally 'They all left the inn together,' indicating a departure or exit.
  • 媪与艳梅叠跨其一 - Means 'The old woman and Yan Mei rode together on one,' describing a shared mode of transportation.
  • 惟学洙另乘 - Literally 'Only Xuezhou rode separately,' indicating a solitary action.
  • 媪叱之 - Means 'The old woman scolded them,' suggesting a rebuke or correction.
  • 冉冉上升，瞬息入云中 - Literally 'They slowly rose, and in an instant entered the clouds,' describing a swift and ethereal ascent.
  • 形若纸鸢 - Means 'Their form was like a kite,' creating a vivid and metaphorical image.
  • 倏不见 - Literally 'Suddenly they were no longer visible,' indicating a sudden disappearance.
  • 男妇聚观何啻千百 - Means 'Hundreds or even thousands of men and women gathered to watch,' describing a large and enthusiastic audience.
  • 行者居者咸下拜 - Literally 'Those who were walking and those who were staying all knelt down,' indicating widespread reverence or respect.
  • 因更里名为“三仙” - Means 'Therefore, the village was renamed 'Three Immortals,' suggesting a change in the place's name due to an event.
  • 与黄梁之镇不朽矣 - Literally 'And it would be immortalized with the town of Huangliang,' indicating a lasting legacy.
  • 信哉，人之可以造命也 - Formal expression meaning 'Indeed, people can shape their destiny,' emphasizing the power of human agency.
  • 艳梅以一念欲殉其夫，而殇子转为老彭矣 - Literally 'Yan Mei, with the intention to sacrifice herself for her husband, saw her deceased child transformed into the ancient sage Lao Peng,' describing a miraculous transformation.
  • 学洙以一事克从其妇，而饿骨不难羽化矣 - Means 'Xuezhou, by doing one thing to follow his wife, saw his starving bones easily transform into a feathered being,' indicating a spiritual or physical metamorphosis.
  • 二蹇并举，何异鸡犬同升 - Literally 'The two donkeys rising together, how different is it from chickens and dogs ascending together,' creating a metaphorical comparison.
  • 茅媪之事虽不经 - Means 'The story of Old Woman Mao is somewhat unbelievable,' suggesting a degree of skepticism or incredulity.
  • 然玉成终始，殊不愧为人师 - Literally 'Yet, in completing the task from start to finish, she was not ashamed to be a teacher,' indicating a sense of fulfillment and honor.
  • 独荐西席者，几夺东床，不免令人不满耳 - Means 'Only the one who recommended the teacher nearly took the bride, which cannot help but be unsatisfactory,' suggesting a sense of disappointment or dissatisfaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三仙 - Refers to the Three Immortals, a concept in Chinese mythology and folklore, often associated with deities or enlightened beings.
  • 黄梁之镇 - Refers to the town of Huangliang, which is associated with the famous Chinese legend of 'The Pillow of Yellow Millet,' where a man dreams of a life of luxury and then wakes up to find that only a few moments have passed in reality.
  • 老彭 - Refers to the ancient sage Lao Peng, a legendary figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with longevity and wisdom.
  • 羽化 - Refers to the process of becoming a celestial being or immortals, a concept in Chinese Daoist and folk beliefs.
  • 鸡犬同升 - Refers to the story of the Eight Immortals crossing the sea, where their pets (a chicken and a dog) also ascend to immortality, symbolizing the idea that even the simplest beings can achieve greatness.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 生聞之心動 - Literally 'the student heard it and was moved,' used to convey a sudden emotional reaction or inner impulse.
  • 遂命仆于近村觅得杯酒 - Compound structure meaning 'then ordered his servant to obtain a cup of wine from a nearby village.' Reflects classical concise, elliptical constructions.
  • 自下骑酹之曰 - 'Got off his horse and poured wine in tribute, saying...' The verb �酹 (lèi) is a classical term for pouring wine as an offering, often in a ritual or memorial context.
  • 予为卿泄此沉冤 - 'I have avenged your deep injustice for you.' The use of 卿 (qīng) is a respectful or affectionate form of 'you,' often used in classical literature to address a spirit or beloved.
  • 语甫已 - 'As soon as the words were finished' or 'the moment the words had been spoken.' 甫 (fǔ) is a classical adverb meaning 'just' or 'only.'
  • 觉襟裾有物，累累下垂 - 'Felt something on his robe, hanging down in a string.' 襟裾 refers to the robe or clothing; 累累 means 'in a series' or 'in clusters.'
  • 回顾之又无所见 - 'Looked back but saw nothing.' A common expression in classical ghost stories or supernatural encounters.
  • 乃复趋乘而前 - 'Then he resumed his ride forward.' 趋 (qū) means to hurry or move quickly.
  • 步履周旋辄如此 - 'Every time he walked, it was the same.' 周旋 can mean 'to move about' or 'to turn around,' and is used here to describe the recurring phenomenon.
  • 及寝，则伏于衾侧，扪之渺然 - 'When he went to sleep, it lay on the side of the bed, but when touched, it was nowhere.' 衾 is a quilt or sleeping robe; 扪之渺然 means 'when touched, it was nowhere to be found.'
  • 生心以为异，而不肯宣 - 'He thought it strange, but did not dare to speak of it.' 宣 here is used in the sense of 'to declare' or 'to speak openly.'
  • 凡数十程皆如之 - 'For dozens of miles, it was the same.' 程 is a unit of distance, often used in ancient Chinese texts to measure travel.
  • 及归见母，室家相聚，因述其前事 - 'When he returned home and saw his mother, the family gathered, and he recounted the previous events.' 室家 is a classical term for family or household.
  • 太夫人与细君莫不称异 - 'The elder lady and his wife both called it strange.' 太夫人 is a respectful term for one's mother or a mother-in-law; 细君 is a respectful term for wife.
  • 细君坐蓐 - 'His wife gave birth.' 坐蓐 is a classical term for childbirth.
  • 生遂宿于精室 - 'He then stayed in the inner chamber.' 精室 refers to a private or inner room, often used in classical literature to denote a secluded or sacred space.
  • 夜已过半，闻榻前窸窣有声 - 'The night had passed half, and he heard a rustling sound by the bed.' 窃窣 (xīsū) is an onomatopoeic word for a soft, rustling sound.
  • 生素向慕，竟不之惧 - 'He had long admired her, and so did not fear her.' 素向慕 is 'had always admired'; 竟不之惧 is a classical construction where 之 is the object of 惧 (fear), and the structure is inverted for emphasis.
  • 暮夜无烛，安知非汝以绐我者？ - 'In the dark night without a lamp, how can I know it is not you deceiving me?' 绐 (dài) means to deceive or trick.
  • 灯光四射，烬者复燃 - 'The lights shone in all directions, and the extinguished ones reignited.' 烬者 is 'those that had gone out.'
  • 花貌嫣然，容妆富丽，迥异梦中之惨淡 - 'Her face was beautiful and smiling, her appearance and makeup rich and splendid, greatly different from the gloom of the dream.' 花貌 is a poetic term for a beautiful face; 嫣然 is a smile or rosy expression; 容妆 refers to appearance and makeup; 迥异 means 'greatly different'; 惨淡 is 'dreary' or 'gloomy.'

  • 今日盘飧之馈胡为见赐 - 今日 means 'today,' 盘飧之馈 refers to food offerings, 胡为 means 'why,' and 见赐 means 'to be given.' This phrase expresses the speaker's surprise or annoyance at receiving food.

  • 请即璧还，无劳驱斥 - 请即 means 'please immediately,' 璧还 means 'return like a jade disc,' 无劳 means 'do not bother,' and 驱斥 means 'to drive away.' This phrase is a polite way of asking to return something without further action.
  • 言之色甚怒 - 言之 means 'in his words,' and 色甚怒 means 'his face was very angry.' This phrase describes the speaker's angry expression.

  • 妾为人妇，既嫁而未谒舅姑 - 妾 means 'I,' 为人妇 means 'as a wife,' 既嫁 means 'after marriage,' and 未谒舅姑 means 'did not visit the in-laws.' This phrase points out the speaker's failure to visit her in-laws.

  • 今竟杜绝往来，能不令九泉心恫乎 - 今 means 'now,' 竟 means 'finally,' 杜绝往来 means 'to completely cut off contact,' 能不令 means 'how can it not make,' 九泉 means 'the netherworld,' and 心恫 means 'heart grieve.' This phrase expresses the speaker's concern that cutting off contact would cause grief in the afterlife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 二娘 - Refers to a secondary wife or concubine in a polygamous household, a common practice in traditional Chinese society.
  • 叔 - Refers to the younger brother, a familial relationship that plays a significant role in Confucian family ethics.
  • 兄嫂 - Refers to the elder brother and sister-in-law, representing the older generation in the family hierarchy.
  • 苏季子 - A historical reference to Su Qin, a famous纵横家 (strategist) from the Warring States period, often used to symbolize intelligence and strategic thinking.
  • 庙见 - A traditional Chinese custom where a newly married couple visits the ancestral temple to pay respects to the ancestors, symbolizing the integration of the couple into the family lineage.
  • 九泉 - A metaphor for the afterlife or the netherworld, often used in Chinese literature to express concern for the deceased.
  • 张乐设饮 - Refers to setting up music and preparing a feast.

  • 未幾 - Classical expression meaning 'not long after' or 'shortly thereafter,' indicating a brief passage of time.
  • 交章請退 - Compound expression meaning 'submitted joint memorials requesting to resign' or 'submitted multiple petitions requesting to step down.' Reflects the collective action of officials.
  • 願掛冠歸里 - Classical idiom meaning 'to wish to resign and return to one’s hometown,' often used to express a desire to retire from public life.
  • 以避釜鬵之危 - Metaphorical expression. *釜鬵* refers to cooking vessels (frying pan and cauldron), and here it is used to denote a dangerous or perilous situation, likely a political crisis. 'To avoid the danger of the cauldron' is a metaphor for avoiding a dangerous or deadly situation.
  • 其意固在蘭也 - Literally, 'their intent was certainly on Lan,' meaning that the officials’ real concern was the queen (Lan).
  • 寇深矣，可若何 - 'The enemy is deeply entrenched; what can be done?' This is a rhetorical question expressing the gravity of the situation.
  • 割衽席之愛 - *衽席* refers to the bed or marriage bed, symbolizing conjugal love or marital affection. 'To cut off the love of the bedchamber' means to sacrifice marital affection or to part from one’s spouse.
  • 寇早敛甲退矣 - 'The enemy would have long since withdrawn their armor and retreated,' meaning the enemy would have retreated if the queen had been sacrificed.
  • 仍宴于藻香殿，以饯之 - 'Still held a banquet at the Hall of Fragrant Algae to send her off,' referring to a farewell banquet.
  • 我不可以艳妆往 - 'I cannot go in my beautiful makeup,' indicating the queen's modesty or desire to appear humble in her departure.
  • 易之以出 - 'Changed into it and went out,' referring to the queen changing into her old clothes.
  • 王执斝呜咽 - *斝* is an ancient drinking vessel. 'The king held the *jiǎ* and wept,' indicating a solemn and emotional farewell.
  • 欲如向者合欢之宴，岂可得乎 - 'Can we again have a banquet like the previous one of union?' Refers to a previous intimate or marital celebration.
  • 愿妃善事新主，勿以予为念 - 'I wish you to serve your new lord well, and not to think of me,' the king’s farewell words to the queen.
  • 王是何言也！岂犹不谅我心哉 - 'What words are these, Your Majesty! Do you still not understand my heart?' A strong rebuke by the queen.
  • 小童为社稷有此一行 - 'A small child for the state has made this journey,' a humble way of referring to herself, indicating she is acting for the good of the country.
  • 恨不如虞美人刎颈王前，以明己志 - *虞美人* is a historical figure (a concubine of King Huai of Chu who committed suicide by刎颈, or刎neck, to demonstrate her loyalty). This is a metaphor: 'I regret that I cannot be like Lady Yu (虞美人) who刎ed her neck before the king to clarify her loyalty.'
  • 此心可表，异日自知 - 'This heart can be proven; you will understand it in the future,' meaning that the queen’s loyalty will be evident in time.
  • 王勿以汉王好色语妾也 - 'Do not speak to me as if I am the Han King’s concubine who is prone to desire,' a reference to the Han King’s concubines and the queen’s rejection of being seen as a lust object.
  • 王惭，谢兰，虽词色激烈，然已泪溢杯盈 - The king was ashamed and apologized to Lan, though her words and expressions were fierce, yet her tears had already overflowed the cup.
  • 左右皆涕泣不能仰，相对凄然 - All around wept and could not look up, facing each other in sorrow, describing the emotional atmosphere.
  • 离樽莫罄 - 'The farewell cup was not emptied,' meaning the banquet was not fully consumed, symbolizing the lingering sorrow.
  • 兰因起辞行 - 'Lan then rose to take her leave,' indicating the queen’s departure.
  • 妃之往亵体已甚，不可以再辱国君 - 'The queen’s body has already been disgraced in her departure; it cannot be further disgraced by being seen again by the king,' a polite refusal to be seen by the king again.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 明日 - Means 'the next day' or 'tomorrow,' indicating a temporal shift.
  • 卧床不起 - Literally 'lying in bed unable to get up,' a common phrase describing illness or severe weakness.
  • 实无所苦 - Means 'actually suffering no pain' or 'feeling no discomfort,' often used to describe someone who appears sick but is actually fine.
  • 且一男子，谁肯负之于背耶 - A rhetorical question emphasizing the improbability of betrayal, where '且' (furthermore) introduces the question, '一男子' (a man) is the subject, '谁肯' (who would) is the interrogative, and '负之于背' (carry him on one's back) is the action.
  • 益宠遇之 - Means 'even more favored' or 'treated even better,' indicating increased favoritism.
  • 孝廉小愈，耻为戚党笑，秘而不宣 - A compound sentence where '孝廉小愈' (the scholar slightly recovered) is the subject, '耻为戚党笑' (ashamed to be laughed at by relatives and friends) is the reason, and '秘而不宣' (kept secret and not announced) is the action.
  • 寻亦痊可 - Means 'soon also fully recovered,' indicating a quick recovery.
  • 吴仆健后，辄夜出，囊橐充牣，资用丰饶 - A compound sentence where '吴仆健后' (after Wu's servant recovered) is the time frame, '辄夜出' (would often go out at night) is the action, '囊橐充牣' (with bags and pouches full) and '资用丰饶' (with abundant resources) describe the state.
  • 藉主人庇荫，亦无敢言者 - A compound sentence where '藉主人庇荫' (under the protection of the master) is the condition, and '亦无敢言者' (no one dared to speak) is the result.
  • 明年，孝廉将赴公车，以诸仆为无用，独携吴与二僮行 - A compound sentence where '明年' (the next year) is the time frame, '孝廉将赴公车' (the scholar was about to go to the capital) is the main action, '以诸仆为无用' (considering all the servants useless) is the reason, and '独携吴与二僮行' (only took Wu and two boys with him) is the action.
  • 朱提论千，綵缯无算，皆付吴以为心膂 - A compound sentence where '朱提论千' (thousands of zhu coins) and '綵缯无算' (countless colorful silks) are the items, and '皆付吴以为心膂' (all given to Wu to be his trusted companion) is the action.

  • 行未二日程，至某县之野，林深地僻，行旅绝踪 - A compound sentence where '行未二日程' (after traveling for two days), '至某县之野' (arrived at a place in a certain county), '林深地僻' (the forest was deep and the place was remote), and '行旅绝踪' (no travelers were seen) describe the setting.

  • 忽有双舆止于庭 - '忽有双舆' means 'suddenly two sedan chairs appeared,' and '止于庭' means 'stopped in the courtyard,' describing the arrival of the women.
  • 谓仆曰：‘郎未归，新妇来矣’ - '谓仆曰' means 'told the servant,' '郎未归' means 'the husband has not returned,' and '新妇来矣' means 'the new wife has arrived,' indicating the announcement of the woman's arrival.
  • 其母先陈婚约，次为妾言君事，本未綦详 - '其母先陈婚约' means 'her mother first mentioned the marriage agreement,' '次为妾言君事' means 'then told me about your situation,' and '本未綦详' means 'it was not very detailed,' describing the conversation.
  • 且曰：‘郎亦将返，我女从兹累若矣’ - '郎亦将返' means 'your husband will also return,' and '我女从兹累若矣' means 'my daughter will now be a burden to you,' indicating the mother's concern about the future.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝廉 - Refers to a scholar-official, a title given to those who passed the imperial examinations and were appointed to local government positions.
  • 邑宰 - Refers to the local magistrate or county official, responsible for administrative and judicial duties.
  • 细君 - Refers to the wife or a close female relative, often used in classical texts to denote a woman of high status.
  • 好合在迩 - Refers to an upcoming marriage, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 吉期 - Refers to an auspicious date, often determined by astrological calculations.
  • 双舆 - Refers to two sedan chairs, a symbol of high status and wealth.
  • 新妇 - Refers to a newly married woman, often used in classical texts to denote a bride.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝廉 - The scholar-official.
  • 邑宰 - The local magistrate or county official.
  • 细君 - The wife or a close female relative.
  • 新妇 - The newly married woman.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 妾實不解其何自 - Means 'I truly do not understand where he came from,' expressing confusion about the origin of the person.
  • 正盽恍间 - Means 'in a daze' or 'bewildered,' describing a state of confusion or disorientation.
  • 渠既去，吾事济矣 - Means 'since he has gone, my affairs are settled,' indicating relief or resolution.
  • 幸不辱命 - Means 'fortunately, I did not fail the mission,' expressing success in completing a task.
  • 罪人皆得 - Means 'all the criminals were captured,' indicating the successful apprehension of wrongdoers.
  • 启其一 - Means 'opening one of them,' referring to opening a bag or container.
  • 吴夫妇之首，并婢子颅骨俱还 - Means 'the heads of Mr. Wu and Mrs. Wu, along with the skull of the maid, were all returned,' indicating the return of the heads of the criminals and the maid.
  • 妾易男儿妆，从此间夜出 - Means 'I changed into a boy's attire and left here at night,' describing a disguise and nighttime departure.
  • 即往从贼游 - Means 'and immediately went to join the bandits,' indicating joining the bandits.
  • 尽得其详 - Means 'got all the details,' indicating obtaining complete information.
  • 里中无赖 - Means 'disreputable people in the neighborhood,' referring to local troublemakers.
  • 与郎君有郤者 - Means 'those who have grievances against the young master,' indicating personal conflicts.
  • 非积盗也 - Means 'not habitual thieves,' indicating that they are not career criminals.
  • 久居济上 - Means 'long lived in Jining,' indicating a long residence in a specific place.
  • 以御人闻于时 - Means 'famous for their ability to control others at the time,' indicating a reputation for being domineering.
  • 号称吴一椎、马娘子 - Means 'known as Wu Yizhu and Ma Nvzi,' indicating nicknames.
  • 凶暴异常 - Means 'extremely cruel and violent,' describing their character.
  • 近因官司严扑遁迹于此 - Means 'recently, due to strict government crackdowns, they fled here,' indicating a reason for their current location.
  • 群小依之 - Means 'a group of small-time crooks relied on them,' indicating a following.
  • 遂谋鬻身为内应 - Means 'they then planned to sell themselves as an inside contact,' indicating a scheme.
  • 而君乃受其荼毒 - Means 'and you suffered under their tyranny,' indicating the victim's suffering.
  • 妾知其实 - Means 'I knew their true intentions,' indicating awareness of their plans.
  • 究未悉渠魁之所在 - Means 'but did not know exactly where the ringleaders were,' indicating a lack of complete information.
  • 因以剑术动群贼 - Means 'so I used my sword skills to impress the bandits,' indicating a method of gaining trust.
  • 浼其接引 - Means 'asked them to guide me,' indicating a request for assistance.
  • 俱大悦 - Means 'they were all very pleased,' indicating a positive reaction.
  • 即令一贼导妾至一坟庄 - Means 'they immediately sent one of the bandits to lead me to a tomb complex,' indicating a specific action.
  • 则吴与马及二僮共欢饮 - Means 'Wu, Ma, and the two servants were drinking together,' indicating a scene of revelry.
  • 妾试之以剑 - Means 'I tested them with my sword,' indicating an attempt to assess their abilities.
  • 三寇皆噎 - Means 'the three bandits all choked,' indicating a surprising or unexpected outcome.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红线者流 - Refers to a legendary female assassin known as Hongxian, indicating a reference to a famous figure.
  • 济上 - Refers to Jining, a city in Shandong Province, indicating a specific location.
  • 吴一椎、马娘子 - Refers to the nicknames of the bandit leaders, indicating their reputations.
  • 群小 - Refers to small-time crooks or minor criminals, indicating a social group.

Proper Nouns

[707]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 時已孟秋 - Classical expression meaning 'It was already the first month of autumn'; *孟秋* refers to the first lunar month of autumn (July in the traditional Chinese calendar).
  • 孝廉 - A classical title referring to a scholar-official candidate selected through the imperial examination system; a formal designation for someone recommended for official service.
  • 場期甚迫 - Phrase meaning 'the examination period was very urgent,' indicating the pressure of an upcoming imperial exam.
  • 不赴京 - 'Did not go to the capital,' referring to the capital city where the imperial examinations were held.
  • 使入訪問顧母之所在 - 'Sent someone to inquire about the location of his mother-in-law.' The structure is elliptical and requires contextual interpretation.
  • 踪兆俱渺 - 'All traces and signs were lost,' indicating a complete lack of information or leads.
  • 腼然不答 - 'Shyly did not answer,' suggesting a reluctance or embarrassment.
  • 邂逅一婦 - 'Met a woman by chance,' indicating a fortuitous or unexpected meeting.
  • 孔武有力 - 'Strong and powerful,' a classical phrase used to describe physical strength and vigor.
  • 相携以归，结为伉俪 - 'Took her back with him and married her,' a traditional expression for marriage.
  • 期年生一女 - 'After a year, they had a daughter,' a common classical way of expressing time and birth.
  • 嗣因亲族喋喋 - 'Subsequently, because of the constant talk among relatives,' indicating social pressure or gossip.
  • 妇怒化为野熊 - 'The woman, out of anger, transformed into a wild bear,' a fantastical or allegorical transformation.
  • 负女而去 - 'Carried the girl away,' indicating the woman’s departure with the child.
  • 殆有十七岁矣 - 'Is likely seventeen years old now,' a classical expression of estimation.
  • 吻合 - 'Corresponded exactly,' used here to indicate perfect agreement.
  • 大喜 - 'Greatly pleased,' a standard expression of joy.
  • 要以至家 - 'Insisted on going to the house,' indicating a request or demand.
  • 兄妹礼见女 - 'To meet the girl with the formalities of brother and sister,' suggesting a form of respectful introduction.
  • 不拒 - 'Did not refuse,' indicating acceptance or willingness.
  • 归宁 - A classical term referring to a daughter returning to her parents’ home after marriage.
  • 姻戚 - 'In-laws or marital relatives,' a term used to describe the relationship between two families through marriage.
  • 壮心顿灰 - 'His ambitions were suddenly extinguished,' indicating a sudden loss of motivation or hope.
  • 不复干预人事 - 'No longer intervened in human affairs,' suggesting withdrawal from worldly matters.
  • 人亦知其室有剑仙 - 'People also knew that his household had a sword-spirit,' referring to a supernatural or martial figure.
  • 惧不再逞 - 'Afraid that they would not act again,' indicating fear of further supernatural or martial action.
  • 此故明天启五年事也 - 'This is the story of the year Tianqi Five,' a classical way of dating historical events.
  • 至国初 - 'Until the beginning of the nation/state,' referring to the early years of a new dynasty.
  • 须发皓然 - 'White beard and hair,' indicating old age.
  • 精神矍铄 - 'Vital and spirited,' a classical expression for someone who remains mentally alert and physically strong in old age.
  • 马援传不可不读 - 'The biography of Ma Yuan must not be neglected,' referring to a historical figure and his recorded deeds in the *Book of Han*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孝廉 - A title used during the Han and later dynasties for candidates recommended for official service. It was a form of imperial recommendation system.
  • 孟秋 - The first month of autumn in the traditional Chinese lunar calendar, corresponding to July.
  • 天启五年 - The fifth year of the Tianqi era, referring to the reign of the Ming Emperor Tianqi (r. 1621–1627). This is a specific historical date.
  • 马援 - Ma Yuan (d. 49 BCE), a famous Han dynasty general and statesman. His biography is recorded in the *Book of Han* and is often cited for its moral and inspirational content.
  • 剑仙 - A term referring to a supernatural or martial figure with extraordinary abilities, often associated with Daoist or martial traditions.
  • 归宁 - A traditional custom in which a married woman visits her parents’ home, especially after marriage.
  • 姻戚 - A term used to describe the relationship between families connected through marriage, i.e., in-laws.
  • 壮心顿灰 - A phrase used to describe the sudden loss of ambition or motivation, often in the context of personal or political failure.

Proper Nouns

  • 孝廉 - A formal title for a scholar-official candidate, not a personal name.
  • 天启五年 - The year 1625 in the Ming dynasty, under the reign of Emperor Tianqi.
  • 马援 - Ma Yuan (also spelled Ma Yuan or Ma Yuan), a famous Han dynasty general and statesman.
  • 顾 - A surname, likely referring to the mother of the girl (Axi).
  • 阿惜 - Axi, the name of the girl.
  • 剑仙 - A term referring to a supernatural or martial figure, not a specific person.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Formal introductory phrase used by a historian or commentator to preface their remarks.
  • 太史公游侠一传 - Refers to the biography of the wandering knights in Sima Qian's *Records of the Grand Historian*.
  • 误尽多少伟人 - Means 'has misled many great men,' suggesting that the biography has been misleading.
  • 马伏波画虎一语 - Refers to Ma Fu's (Ma Yuan) famous saying about painting a tiger, which is used metaphorically to mean a clear and insightful statement.
  • 如晨钟棒喝，令人猛省 - A Buddhist metaphor meaning 'like the morning bell and the staff strike, making one suddenly wake up,' used to describe a sudden and profound realization.
  • 孝廉以少年多事，屡遭挫辱 - Means 'the scholar-official, being young and full of ambition, has often suffered setbacks and humiliations.'
  • 其不类狗也几希 - Means 'how little he differs from a dog,' a harsh criticism implying that without guidance, he would be no better than a dog.
  • 孝廉之武勇且然，矧夫尔勇伊何者耶 - Means 'even the martial valor of the scholar-official is thus affected, how much more so for those who lack courage,' a rhetorical question emphasizing the severity of the situation.
  • 长途跋涉 - Means 'to travel a long distance with great effort.'
  • 偶经一废寺 - Means 'chanced upon an abandoned temple.'
  • 小憩其中 - Means 'took a short rest there.'
  • 殿宇皆倾圯 - Means 'the halls and buildings were all in ruins.'
  • 瞿昙之像亦约略而知 - Means 'the images of the Buddha were only vaguely recognizable.'
  • 惟两廊屹立，焕然如新 - Means 'only the two side halls stood tall, looking as bright and new as ever.'
  • 壁间绘事绝精 - Means 'the paintings on the walls were extremely exquisite.'
  • 皆奇诡不可测 - Means 'all strange and mysterious, beyond comprehension.'
  • 美妇人浓妆跨虎者 - Describes a woman in heavy makeup riding a tiger.
  • 粉骷髅艳服对镜者 - Describes a skeleton in a beautiful dress looking into a mirror.
  • 缚少年于桐柱而刳其心肝者 - Describes a scene where a young man is tied to a post and his heart and liver are cut out.
  • 捽男子于火床而烙其手足者 - Describes a scene where a man is thrown onto a hot bed and his hands and feet are burned.
  • 以金针剔丈夫之目者 - Describes a scene where a needle is used to prick a man's eyes.
  • 以绣线纫良人之耳者 - Describes a scene where a needle and thread are used to sew a man's ears.
  • 女首蛇身双双而纠缠一雄者 - Describes a scene where a woman with a snake's body and a man are entangled.
  • 狮面狼牙耽耽而词逐一雌者 - Describes a scene where a creature with a lion's face and wolf's teeth is stalking a female.
  • 衣冠者，俱俯耳帖受 - Means 'those in formal attire, all obediently listening.'
  • 巾帼者，率意气自得 - Means 'those in women's attire, all self-assured and proud.'
  • 他如剥肤吸髓，刓肉舔痈，不能悉数 - Describes various other scenes of violence and suffering, too numerous to count.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太史公 - Refers to Sima Qian, the historian who wrote the *Records of the Grand Historian*.
  • 游侠一传 - Refers to the biography of the wandering knights in Sima Qian's work, which has been criticized for its misleading portrayal.
  • 马伏波 - Refers to Ma Yuan, a general known for his wisdom and clear statements.
  • 瞿昙 - Another name for the Buddha.
  • 废寺 - An abandoned temple, often a symbol of decay and neglect in classical Chinese literature.
  • 画廊 - A gallery of paintings, often used to describe a collection of artworks or scenes.

Proper Nouns

  • 太史公 - Sima Qian, the historian.
  • 马伏波 - Ma Yuan, a general.
  • 孝廉 - A scholar-official, a term used to describe a person of high moral and intellectual standing.
  • 瞿昙 - Another name for the Buddha.
  • 废寺 - An abandoned temple.
  • 画廊 - A gallery of paintings.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三世以前为贵官 - Refers to the author's past lives as a high-ranking official.
  • 廉隅自饬 - Means 'to discipline oneself in terms of integrity and propriety.'
  • 声色犹未能远 - Literally 'still not able to distance oneself from sensual pleasures,' indicating a struggle to avoid worldly desires.
  • 误出人死罪 - Means 'mistakenly causing someone's death by a capital offense.'
  • 投间 - Literally 'to be framed or implicated,' suggesting being falsely accused or involved in a scandal.
  • 归田后 - Means 'after returning to the countryside,' referring to retirement or withdrawal from public life.
  • 过听姬言 - Means 'overhearing the words of a concubine,' indicating being misled by someone close to him.
  • 强预一讼事 - Means 'forcibly getting involved in a lawsuit,' suggesting an inappropriate or ill-advised legal intervention.
  • 遭剥面之辱 - Literally 'suffered the disgrace of being disfigured,' referring to a humiliating and physical punishment.
  • 心窃衔之 - Means 'secretly harboring resentment,' indicating a hidden grudge.
  • 一笑回嗔 - Means 'a smile that turns away anger,' describing a moment of forgiveness or reconciliation.
  • 荏苒遂与之私 - Literally 'gradually and secretly becoming intimate,' referring to a clandestine relationship.
  • 夜无虚夕 - Means 'no night without intimacy,' emphasizing the frequency of their encounters.
  • 斫丧过多 - Literally 'wasting too much energy,' referring to physical or emotional exhaustion.
  • 患痨瘵不起 - Means 'suffering from consumption (tuberculosis) and unable to recover,' indicating a fatal illness.
  • 床褥之间，犹以情词往来 - Literally 'even in bed, exchanging affectionate words,' describing continued emotional connection despite physical decline.
  • 赍恨而亡 - Means 'dying with a grudge,' indicating a lingering resentment at the time of death.
  • 今世为僧 - Means 'in this life as a monk,' referring to the author's current reincarnation.
  • 童年随父远涉江湖 - Means 'as a child, traveling far with his father,' suggesting a wandering or adventurous youth.
  • 邂逅一女鬼 - Means 'encountering a female ghost,' referring to a supernatural encounter.
  • 相随不去 - Means 'staying with him,' indicating the ghost's persistent presence.
  • 油枯灯尽 - Literally 'oil exhausted, lamp extinguished,' metaphorically referring to the end of life or vitality.
  • 赖吾师以药投之 - Means 'relying on my teacher to administer medicine,' suggesting a miraculous or divine intervention.
  • 甫得更生 - Means 'just barely revived,' indicating a narrow escape from death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三世 - Refers to the concept of reincarnation in Buddhist or Taoist thought, suggesting the author's belief in past lives.
  • 贵官 - Refers to high-ranking officials in the imperial bureaucracy, reflecting the social hierarchy of the time.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 即如翁之明虔 - Formal expression meaning 'as for the old man's devoutness.' Emphasizes sincerity and reverence.

  • 亲诣坛眀 - Means 'to personally visit the altar.' The term *坛眀* (tán míng) likely refers to a sacred or ritual space.
  • 予始降若以福哉 - Formal expression meaning 'I would then descend upon you with blessings.' Suggests divine intervention or blessing.
  • 色然而骇 - Means 'his face turned pale with fright.' Conveys a strong emotional reaction.
  • 遁迹于斯 - Literally 'to hide one's traces here,' suggesting a retreat or concealment.
  • 托故至者 - Means 'those who come under pretenses.' The term *托故* (tuō gù) implies ulterior motives.
  • 不尽攄诚 - Means 'not fully expressing sincerity.' The term *攄诚* (shū chéng) refers to pouring out one's sincerity or devotion.
  • 冥冥之中既已洞鉴 - Means 'in the unseen realm, it has already seen through everything.' Suggests omniscient knowledge.
  • 嘈杂扰攘 - Means 'noisy and disruptive.' Describes a chaotic and distracting environment.
  • 大溃男女之防 - Means 'greatly breaking down the barriers between men and women.' Suggests violation of social norms.
  • 废公私之务 - Means 'wasting public and private affairs.' Implies detrimental impact on both official and personal matters.
  • 村野之夫 - Means 'a rustic man.' Describes a simple, unrefined individual.
  • 汗出如蒸 - Means 'sweating profusely.' Vividly describes the intensity of the heat.
  • 衣湿如浣 - Means 'clothes as wet as if washed.' Emphasizes the degree of wetness.
  • 经年不濯之垢 - Means 'dirt accumulated over years without washing.' Highlights the dirtiness of the clothes.
  • 郁积而升 - Means 'accumulating and rising.' Describes the buildup of unpleasant odors.
  • 薰以荀令之香 - Means 'perfumed with the fragrance of Xun Ling.' Refers to a famous perfume, used ironically to contrast with unpleasant odors.
  • 闻者亦罔不掩鼻 - Means 'those who hear it also cannot help but cover their noses.' Emphasizes the strong and unpleasant smell.
  • 入喉三寸 - Means 'three inches into the throat.' Hyperbolic description of the depth of a smell.
  • 酒蒜之臭犹存 - Means 'the smell of wine and garlic still lingers.' Describes lingering odor of food.
  • 果腹崇朝 - Means 'to fill one's stomach in the morning.' Describes the act of eating.
  • 谷食之性渐化 - Means 'the nature of grains and food gradually transforms.' Suggests the digestive process.
  • 口不能缄 - Means 'cannot keep one's mouth closed.' Implies people cannot stop talking or complaining.
  • 脔不能掩 - Means 'cannot cover the pieces of meat.' Suggests pervasive and un concealable unpleasant odors.
  • 氤氲而来者 - Means 'those who come with a pervasive smell.' Describes persistent and unpleasant odor.
  • 诚有之 - Means 'truly there is such a thing.' Acknowledges the existence of the described phenomenon.
  • 未也 - Means 'not yet.' Indicates the situation is not fully resolved.
  • 是犹可耐者 - Means 'this is still bearable.' Suggests the situation is tolerable but not ideal.

[724]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Common phrase in Classical Chinese, often used to introduce a commentary or moral reflection. Can be translated as 'The historian says' or 'The commentator remarks.'
  • 專好尋神入廟 - Literally means 'exclusively fond of seeking gods in temples.' This phrase is idiomatic and suggests a tendency to seek spiritual or divine intervention without considering the consequences.
  • 不虞神之攒眉已久也 - 'Not realizing that the gods have been frowning for a long time.' This is a metaphorical expression indicating that the gods are displeased or unhappy.
  • 舉大会、賽大社 - Refers to large gatherings or festivals, often religious or communal in nature.
  • 呼姨唤姊引女随娘攘攘而来者 - A vivid description of a crowd of women and girls coming together, possibly for a festival or event. '攘攘' means bustling or crowded.
  • 苎萝西子 - A reference to Xi Shi, a famous beauty from ancient China, often used to denote great beauty.
  • 蒙不洁 - Means 'to be defiled' or 'to be impure.'
  • 阴浊之气、妖冶之形 - Refers to a dark, turbid atmosphere and an alluring, seductive appearance, respectively.
  • 阳明正直之神 - Refers to the pure and upright deities.
  • 屏息而避之 - Means 'to hold one's breath and avoid it,' indicating a strong aversion or fear.
  • 于以邀福，不綦难乎 - 'To seek blessings, is it not extremely difficult?' This is a rhetorical question emphasizing the difficulty of achieving blessings under such conditions.
  • 昔京西有异僧 - 'In the past, there was a strange monk in the western part of the capital.'
  • 斫二石球 - 'Cut two stone balls,' referring to the creation of stone balls by the monk.
  • 晨则登绝高之峰，自崖而推之，径达其麓 - Describes the monk's daily ritual of climbing a high peak, rolling the stone balls down the cliff, and watching them reach the foot of the mountain.
  • 已乃下而掇石，再登再推，操纵终日 - 'Then he would descend, pick up the stones, climb again, and push them again, controlling them all day long.'
  • 人因号之曰魔 - 'People therefore called him a demon,' indicating the monk's unconventional and possibly unsettling behavior.
  • 而钦其道行者来且接踵 - 'And those who admired his spiritual practice came in a continuous stream.'
  • 僧于丈夫犹见礼，独至巾帼者流则谩骂曰 - 'The monk treated men with respect, but when it came to women, he would curse them, saying...' indicating the monk's different treatment of men and women.
  • 没家法！露面出头，直供妄男子看杀耳，岂真为老僧哉！ - 'No family discipline! Showing your face and head, you are just for the sake of being looked at by foolish men, not truly for the old monk!' The monk's curse criticizing the lack of proper conduct among women.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - A term used in Chinese literature to denote a historian or commentator, often appearing in classical texts to provide commentary or moral reflections.
  • 京西 - Refers to the western part of the capital, likely referring to the imperial city of the time.
  • 异僧 - A strange or unusual monk, often used to describe someone with unconventional or mystical practices.
  • 石球 - Stone balls, which in this context are used in a ritual or performance by the monk.
  • 绝高之峰 - A very high peak, emphasizing the monk's physical and spiritual challenges.
  • 麓 - The base or foot of a mountain.
  • 巾帼 - Refers to women, often used in a collective sense.
  • 家法 - Family discipline or rules, here used metaphorically to criticize the lack of proper conduct among women.

  • 京西 - The western part of the capital.
  • 异僧 - A strange or unusual monk.
  • 石球 - Stone balls.
  • 绝高之峰 - A very high peak.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 智媪 - Title of the story, meaning 'The Wise Old Woman.' The character '○' is likely a decorative or structural marker used in classical texts.
  • 舊傳 - Classical term meaning 'it is said in old accounts' or 'it is recorded in ancient traditions.' Introduces the narrative as a traditional or legendary tale.
  • 蓋富家也 - Classical construction meaning 'was likely a wealthy family.' The word '蓋' functions as a modal particle, suggesting a probable or inferred statement.
  • 劇盜 - Term for 'notorious bandits' or 'dangerous robbers.' '劇' implies intensity or danger.
  • 聚黨十數 - Literally 'gathered a group of ten or so,' meaning the bandits had a small gang of about ten members.

  • 不必玉软香温，惟虑迷桃源之路 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'not necessarily needing softness and warmth, but only worrying about being lost on the path to Peach Blossom Spring.'

  • 迨至理绣窗前，小试鸠盘之毒手 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'when it comes to the embroidered window, trying out the poisonous hand of the magpie's nest' (a euphemism for infidelity).
  • 渐且合欢床上，痛遭鬼母之沉椎 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'gradually on the bed of joy, suffering the heavy blows of the ghost mother' (a euphemism for severe punishment).
